# numopt4j

> High-performance Java numerical optimization library providing L-BFGS-B, SLSQP, TRF, and root-finding algorithms.

numopt4j is a Java numerical optimization library supporting unconstrained optimization, bound-constrained optimization, equality/inequality constrained optimization, nonlinear least-squares fitting, and root finding.

## Algorithm Selection

- Unconstrained or bound-constrained optimization → L-BFGS-B (`Minimizer.lbfgsb()`)
- Equality or inequality constrained optimization → SLSQP (`Minimizer.slsqp()`)
- Nonlinear least-squares fitting → TRF (`Minimizer.trf()`)
- Scalar root-finding → Brentq (`RootFinder.brentq()`)
- Multivariate root-finding with Jacobian → HYBR (`RootFinder.hybr()`)
- Multivariate root-finding Jacobian-free → Broyden (`RootFinder.broyden()`)
- Matrix decompositions → `Decomposer` facade (LU, QR, LQ, SVD, Cholesky, Schur, Eigen, GEVD, GGEVD, GSVD)

## Package Structure

```
com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim              # Minimizer, RootFinder, Bound, OptimizationResult, OptimizationStatus, OptimizationException
com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.lbfgsb      # LBFGSBProblem, LBFGSBWorkspace
com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.slsqp       # SLSQPProblem, SLSQPWorkspace
com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.trf         # TRFProblem, TRFWorkspace
com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.root        # BrentqProblem, HYBRProblem, HYBRWorkspace, BroydenProblem, BroydenWorkspace
com.curioloop.numopt4j.linalg            # Decomposer, Decomposition
com.curioloop.numopt4j.linalg.mat        # LU, QR, LQ, SVD, Cholesky, Schur, Eigen, GEVD, GGEVD, GSVD
```

Typical import for optimization:
```java
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.lbfgsb.*;
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.slsqp.*;
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.trf.*;
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.root.*;
```

Typical import for matrix decompositions:
```java
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.linalg.*;
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.linalg.mat.*;
```

## Quick Start

### Bound-constrained optimization (L-BFGS-B)

```java
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.lbfgsb.*;

OptimizationResult r = Minimizer.lbfgsb()
    .objective(x -> x[0]*x[0] + x[1]*x[1])
    .bounds(Bound.atLeast(0.5), Bound.atLeast(0.5))
    .initialPoint(1.0, 1.0)
    .solve();

if (r.isSuccessful()) {
    System.out.println(Arrays.toString(r.getSolution()));
} else {
    System.out.println(r.getErrorMessage());
}
```

### Equality-constrained optimization (SLSQP)

```java
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.slsqp.*;

// minimize x[0] + x[1], subject to x[0]^2 + x[1]^2 = 1
OptimizationResult r = Minimizer.slsqp()
    .objective(x -> x[0] + x[1])
    .equalityConstraints(x -> x[0]*x[0] + x[1]*x[1] - 1)
    .initialPoint(0.5, 0.5)
    .solve();
```

### Nonlinear least squares (TRF)

```java
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.trf.*;

OptimizationResult r = Minimizer.trf()
    .residuals((x, res) -> {
        res[0] = x[0] - 3.0;
        res[1] = x[1] - 4.0;
    }, 2)
    .initialPoint(0.0, 0.0)
    .solve();
```

### Scalar root-finding (Brentq)

```java
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.root.*;

// Find root of sin(x) in [3, 4] → π
OptimizationResult r = RootFinder.brentq(Math::sin)
    .bracket(Bound.between(3.0, 4.0))
    .solve();
double root = r.getRoot(); // ≈ π
```

### Multivariate root-finding (HYBR / Broyden)

```java
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.numopt4j.optim.root.*;

// HYBR (Powell hybrid, requires Jacobian — numerical by default)
OptimizationResult r = RootFinder.hybr((x, f) -> {
        f[0] = x[0]*x[0] - 2;
        f[1] = x[1] - x[0];
    }, 2)
    .initialPoint(1.0, 1.0)
    .solve();
double[] solution = r.getSolution(); // [√2, √2]

// Broyden (Jacobian-free)
RootFinder.broyden(fn, 2).initialPoint(1.0, 1.0).solve();

// Central-difference Jacobian (HYBR only)
RootFinder.hybr(fn, 2).jacobian(NumericalJacobian.CENTRAL).initialPoint(1.0, 1.0).solve();
```

## Core API

### Minimizer (facade — static factory entry point)

```java
Minimizer.lbfgsb()   // → LBFGSBProblem
Minimizer.slsqp()    // → SLSQPProblem
Minimizer.trf()      // → TRFProblem
```

### LBFGSBProblem (L-BFGS-B bound-constrained quasi-Newton)

```java
Minimizer.lbfgsb()
    .objective(ToDoubleFunction<double[]>)  // auto-uses NumericalGradient.CENTRAL
    .objective(Univariate)                  // analytic gradient
    .initialPoint(double...)                // sets x₀ and infers dimension
    .bounds(Bound...)                       // optional, lb ≤ x ≤ ub
    .maxIterations(int)                     // default 15000
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 15000
    .gradientTolerance(double)              // default 1e-5
    .functionTolerance(double)              // default 1e7 * ε
    .corrections(int)                       // L-BFGS memory parameter m, default 10
    .maxLineSearchSteps(int)                // default 20
    .solve()                                // → OptimizationResult
    .alloc()                                // → LBFGSBWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### SLSQPProblem (SLSQP sequential least-squares programming)

```java
Minimizer.slsqp()
    .objective(ToDoubleFunction<double[]> / Univariate)
    .equalityConstraints(ToDoubleFunction<double[]>...)   // c(x) = 0
    .inequalityConstraints(ToDoubleFunction<double[]>...) // c(x) >= 0
    .initialPoint(double...)
    .bounds(Bound...)
    .functionTolerance(double)              // default 1e-6
    .maxIterations(int)                     // default 100
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 1000
    .solve()                                // → OptimizationResult
    .alloc()                                // → SLSQPWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### TRFProblem (TRF trust-region reflective least squares)

```java
Minimizer.trf()
    .residuals(BiConsumer<double[],double[]>, int m)  // auto-uses NumericalJacobian.CENTRAL
    .objective(Multivariate)                          // analytic Jacobian
    .initialPoint(double...)
    .bounds(Bound...)
    .functionTolerance(double)              // default 1e-8
    .parameterTolerance(double)             // default 1e-8
    .gradientTolerance(double)              // default 1e-8
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 100 * n
    .loss(RobustLoss)                       // default LINEAR
    .lossScale(double)                      // soft margin C, default 1.0
    .solve()                                // → OptimizationResult
    .alloc()                                // → TRFWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### RootFinder (facade — static factory entry point)

```java
RootFinder.brentq(DoubleUnaryOperator f)                    // → BrentqProblem
RootFinder.hybr(BiConsumer<double[],double[]> fn, int n)    // → HYBRProblem
RootFinder.broyden(BiConsumer<double[],double[]> fn, int n) // → BroydenProblem
```

### BrentqProblem (1-D Brent's method)

```java
RootFinder.brentq(DoubleUnaryOperator f)
    .bracket(Bound.between(a, b))       // required: f(a)*f(b) <= 0
    .absoluteTolerance(double)          // default 2e-12
    .relativeTolerance(double)          // default 4 * ε
    .maxIterations(int)
    .solve()                            // → OptimizationResult
```

### HYBRProblem (N-D Powell hybrid, MINPACK hybrd)

```java
RootFinder.hybr(BiConsumer<double[],double[]> fn, int n)
    .jacobian(NumericalJacobian)    // default FORWARD
    .functionTolerance(double)      // default 1.49e-8
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 200*(n+1)
    .initialPoint(double...)        // required
    .solve()                        // → OptimizationResult
    .alloc()                        // → HYBRWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### BroydenProblem (N-D Broyden's method, Jacobian-free)

```java
RootFinder.broyden(BiConsumer<double[],double[]> fn, int n)
    .functionTolerance(double)      // default 1.49e-8
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 100*(n+1)
    .initialPoint(double...)        // required
    .solve()                        // → OptimizationResult
    .alloc()                        // → BroydenWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### OptimizationResult

```java
result.isSuccessful()       // whether converged
result.getRoot()            // 1-D root value (NaN for N-D)
result.getSolution()        // solution vector (null if not set)
result.getCost()            // objective / residual norm value at termination
result.getIterations()      // iteration count
result.getEvaluations()     // function evaluation count
result.getSummary()         // single-line summary string
result.getErrorMessage()    // reason + suggestion on failure, null otherwise
result.toString()           // multi-line structured diagnostic output
```

### Bound

```java
Bound.unbounded()           // x ∈ (-∞, +∞), no constraint
Bound.between(lower, upper) // lower ≤ x ≤ upper
Bound.atLeast(lower)        // x ≥ lower
Bound.atMost(upper)         // x ≤ upper
Bound.exactly(value)        // x = value (fixed variable)
Bound.nonNegative()         // x ≥ 0
Bound.nonPositive()         // x ≤ 0
new Bound(lower, upper)     // public constructor; use Double.NaN for no bound on that side (for serialization / reflection)
```

### NumericalGradient

| Method | Accuracy | Evals/dim |
|--------|----------|-----------|
| `FORWARD` | O(h) | 1 |
| `BACKWARD` | O(h) | 1 |
| `CENTRAL` | O(h²) — default | 2 |
| `FIVE_POINT` | O(h⁴) | 4 |

### NumericalJacobian

| Method | Accuracy | Evals |
|--------|----------|-------|
| `FORWARD` | O(h) | n+1 |
| `CENTRAL` | O(h²) — default | 2n |

## Decomposer (Matrix Decompositions)

`com.curioloop.numopt4j.linalg.Decomposer`

Unified facade for all matrix decompositions. All methods accept an optional `Pool` for workspace reuse and optional `Opts` enums. Input matrices are overwritten in place (row-major `double[]`).

### Decomposition Selection

| Problem | Class | Entry point |
|---|---|---|
| Solve Ax=b, inverse, determinant | `LU` | `Decomposer.lu(A, n)` |
| Least squares, rank, solve overdetermined | `QR` | `Decomposer.qr(A, m, n)` |
| Minimum-norm solution (m≤n) | `LQ` | `Decomposer.lq(A, m, n)` |
| Singular values, low-rank approx, pseudoinverse | `SVD` | `Decomposer.svd(A, m, n)` |
| Symmetric positive-definite solve | `Cholesky` | `Decomposer.cholesky(A, n)` |
| Eigenvalues of general or symmetric matrix | `Eigen` | `Decomposer.eigen(A, n)` |
| Schur form, Lyapunov equations | `Schur` | `Decomposer.schur(A, n)` |
| Generalized eigen, A sym + B sym pos-def | `GEVD` | `Decomposer.gevd(A, B, n)` |
| Generalized eigen, A and B general | `GGEVD` | `Decomposer.ggevd(A, B, n)` |
| Generalized SVD of two matrices | `GSVD` | `Decomposer.gsvd(A, m, n, B, p)` |

### Quick Reference

```java
// LU — solve, inverse, determinant
LU lu = Decomposer.lu(A, n);
lu.solve(b, x);          // solve Ax = b
lu.inverse(Ainv);        // compute A⁻¹
lu.determinant();        // det(A)
lu.cond();               // condition number
lu.toL(); lu.toU(); lu.toP();

// QR — least squares, rank-revealing
QR qr = Decomposer.qr(A, m, n);                        // standard
QR qrp = Decomposer.qr(A, m, n, QR.Opts.PIVOTING);     // rank-revealing
qr.solve(b, x);          // solve square system
qr.leastSquares(b, x);   // min‖Ax-b‖
qr.rank();               // numerical rank (pivoting mode)
qr.toQ(); qr.toR(); qr.toP();

// SVD — singular values, pseudoinverse
SVD svd = Decomposer.svd(A, m, n);                      // thin U and Vᵀ
SVD svdF = Decomposer.svd(A, m, n, SVD.Opts.FULL_U, SVD.Opts.FULL_V);
svd.singularValues();    // double[] s
svd.solve(b, x);         // pseudoinverse solve
svd.rank(); svd.cond();
svd.toU(); svd.toVT();

// Cholesky — symmetric positive-definite
Cholesky ch = Decomposer.cholesky(A, n);                // standard
Cholesky ldl = Decomposer.cholesky(A, n, Cholesky.Opts.PIVOTING); // LDLᵀ
ch.solve(b, x);
ch.determinant(); ch.logDet(); ch.cond();
ch.toL(); ldl.toD();     // D only for LDLᵀ

// Eigen — symmetric or general
Eigen es = Decomposer.eigen(A, n, Eigen.Opts.SYMMETRIC_LOWER);
Eigen eg = Decomposer.eigen(A, n);                      // general, right vectors
Eigen el = Decomposer.eigen(A, n, Eigen.Opts.WANT_LEFT, Eigen.Opts.WANT_RIGHT);
es.eigenvalues();        // double[] (real, ascending for symmetric)
eg.eigenvalues();        // double[] real parts; eigenvaluesImag() for imaginary
es.toV();                // n×n eigenvector matrix
es.toS();                // n×1 eigenvalue matrix (complex for general)

// Schur — A = Z·T·Zᵀ, Lyapunov solvers
Schur sc = Decomposer.schur(A, n);
Schur scLHP = Decomposer.schur(A, n, Schur.Opts.SORT_LHP); // sort stable eigenvalues first
sc.toT(); sc.toZ(); sc.toS();
sc.lyapunov(Q);          // AX + XAᵀ = Q
sc.discreteLyapunov(A, Q);  // AXAᵀ - X = Q

// GEVD — generalized symmetric-definite: A·x = λ·B·x
// Requires A symmetric, B symmetric positive-definite
GEVD gv = Decomposer.gevd(A, B, n);                     // type 1, lower triangle
GEVD gv2 = Decomposer.gevd(A, B, n, GEVD.Opts.UPPER, GEVD.Opts.TYPE2);
gv.eigenvalues();        // double[] (real, ascending)
gv.toV();                // n×n eigenvector matrix
gv.toS();                // n×1 eigenvalue matrix

// GGEVD — generalized non-symmetric: A·x = λ·B·x
// A and B are general matrices; eigenvalues may be complex
GGEVD gg = Decomposer.ggevd(A, B, n);
gg.alphar(); gg.alphai(); gg.beta();  // eigenvalue = (alphar+i*alphai)/beta
gg.eigenvalues();        // double[2n] interleaved (re,im) pairs
gg.toS();                // n×1 complex matrix
gg.toVR(); gg.toVL();    // right/left eigenvector matrices

// GSVD — generalized SVD of A (m×n) and B (p×n)
GSVD gs = Decomposer.gsvd(A, m, n, B, p);
gs.sigma();              // generalized singular values
gs.toU(); gs.toV(); gs.toQ();
gs.rank(); gs.cond();

// Workspace reuse
LU.Pool ws = Decomposer.lu(A, n).pool();
for (double[] mat : matrices) {
    LU result = Decomposer.lu(mat, n, ws);
}
```

## Documentation

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): Full API reference (for deep AI understanding)
- [GitHub Wiki](https://github.com/curioloop/numopt4j/wiki/): Detailed error code fix guides
